input.background: Yay-Sachs is a metabolic disorder that is inherited as an autosomal recessive trait. Both recessive alleles are necessary for expression of the disease. Therefore, when each parent is a carrier, there is a one in four chance of transmitting the genetic disorder to each offspring.
input.question: Let X represent the number of offspring affected in three consecutive conceptions from Tay-Sachs carrier parents. Is X a binomial random variable? Explain your response.

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性
output.answer: Yes, this is a binomial random variable because it is based on $n=3$ independent Bernoulli trials, each with probability of success $p=0.25$.

X.description: The number of offspring affected by Tay-Sachs disease in three consecutive conceptions from carrier parents.

Probability of Transmission.description: The probability of transmitting the Tay-Sachs genetic disorder to each offspring when both parents are carriers.

Number of Trials.description: The number of consecutive conceptions from Tay-Sachs carrier parents.
